meta.name: python
meta.title: Python Skill
meta.description: Python coding conventions and guidelines Triggers on: **/*.py

original_definition.skill_description: Python coding conventions and guidelines Triggers on: **/*.py

actions.name: Write clear and concise comments for each function.
actions.description: Write clear and concise comments for each function.

actions.name: Write code with good maintainability practices, including co
actions.description: Write code with good maintainability practices, including comments on why certain design decisions were made.

actions.name: Handle edge cases and write clear exception handling.
actions.description: Handle edge cases and write clear exception handling.

actions.name: Write concise, efficient, and idiomatic code that is also ea
actions.description: Write concise, efficient, and idiomatic code that is also easily understandable.

actions.name: Write unit tests for functions and document them with docstr
actions.description: Write unit tests for functions and document them with docstrings explaining the test cases.

rules.description: Ensure functions have descriptive names and include type hints.
Ensure lines do not exceed 79 characters.
Always include test cases for critical paths of the application.

directives.description: Use the `typing` module for type annotations (e.g., `List[str]`, `Dict[str, int]`).
Break down complex functions into smaller, more manageable functions.
For algorithm-related code, include explanations of the approach used.
For libraries or external dependencies, mention their usage and purpose in comments.
Use consistent naming conventions and follow language-specific best practices.
Maintain proper indentation (use 4 spaces for each level of indentation).
Place function and class docstrings immediately after the `def` or `class` keyword.
Use blank lines to separate functions, classes, and code blocks where appropriate.
Account for common edge cases like empty inputs, invalid data types, and large datasets.
Include comments for edge cases and the expected behavior in those cases.
